column1: question
Classify the different types of system calls with suitable examples.
Summarize the concept of process scheduling and its importance in system performance.
Apply operating system operations to explain how processes are executed and managed in a system.
Apply the functions of an operating system to illustrate how it manages hardware and software resources in a computing environment.
Draw the Gantt chart showing process execution.
Calculate the waiting time for each process.
Calculate the turnaround time for each process.
Compute the average waiting time and average turnaround time.
Determine the completion time for each process.
A system administrator changes the scheduling algorithm from FCFS to Round Robin. Analyze the impact of this change on response time and fairness.
A real-time system experiences delays due to improper CPU allocation. Analyze the scheduling criteria and determine which factor (waiting time, turnaround time, response time) is most impacted.
Two processes use semaphores to share a printer. Apply wait() and signal() to ensure no process prints at the same time.
Given two programs, one using contiguous allocation and one using paging, compare their memory efficiency and fragmentation issues.
In a memory allocation scenario with fragmentation, apply paging to eliminate external fragmentation.
Apply the concept of demand paging to illustrate how pages are loaded into memory during program execution.
In a shared buffer system, implement synchronization using semaphores to avoid data corruption.
A student creates a multi-threaded chat app. Apply mutex locks to handle simultaneous message writes.
What is the content of a matrix NEED?
Is the system in Safe state? If yes, give the safe state.
If a request from a process P1 arrives for (0,4,2,0), can the request be granted immediately?
Analyze the system by computing the Need matrix.
Apply a suitable algorithm that determines whether the system is in a safe state.
If the system is safe, derive a safe sequence; otherwise, justify why the system is unsafe.
